search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng152 leg ulcer infection reassessment signs referral
candidate_sources_rejected: NICE antibiotic tables and dosage schedules
general wound-care pages without an exact leg-ulcer section
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: No medicine, dose, duration, automatic culture, referral, or escalation was mapped.
Broad wound-care material was not used in place of the exact leg-ulcer recommendations.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with a leg ulcer; arterial, venous, diabetic, pressure, inflammatory, malignant, traumatic, pediatric, and pregnancy causes require separate assessment and evidence.
setting_applicability: UAE primary care, community wound care, vascular, diabetes, dermatology, and hospital review.
UAE_applicability: NICE assessment principles are transferable, but UAE wound, vascular, diabetes, microbiology, antimicrobial, facility, and referral pathways remain local.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG152 recommendations were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NICE NG152 remains the current official guideline identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact leg-ulcer section supports documenting interval course, symptoms, functional effect, site, surrounding skin, warmth, spreading redness or swelling, pain, fever, underlying-condition and prior-antibiotic context without diagnosing infection.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting underlying-condition context and clinician-assessed redness or swelling beyond the ulcer, warmth, increased pain, fever, severity, complication risk, and previous antibiotic exposure.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The reassessment section supports recording worsening, non-improvement, systemic illness, disproportionate pain, previous antibiotic exposure and microbiology results already reviewed; no test or treatment is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting rapid worsening, non-improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, previous antibiotics, microbiology sampling already performed, and results already reviewed.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The referral section supports documenting clinician-discussed safety net and an arranged review or completed specialist discussion; it does not create referral or urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-identified serious illness, diabetes, immunosuppression, lymphangitis, spreading infection, or completed referral or specialist discussion; it does not create the referral.

unresolved_source_gaps: Ulcer cause, exact site, dimensions, depth, wound bed, exudate, odour, pulses, perfusion, oedema, neuropathy, diabetes status, dressings, healing trajectory, diagnosis, vascular tests, treatment, compression, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
